evals.prompt: Ship it. I just finished adding webhook retry logic to my Go CLI tool. The repo has Release Please, GoReleaser, and install scripts. There's an open issue #14 about webhook reliability.
evals.expected_output: Full pipeline execution: tests with coverage, code review, PII scan, conventional commit review, push, PR creation with 'Closes #14', release monitoring, artifact install via install script, validation, issue #14 closure with notes.

assertions.text: Gathers git state, project type, release pipeline, and issue context before asking any questions
Asks all clarification questions in a single batch
Reviews commit messages for conventional format
Monitors Release Please pipeline for release

evals.prompt: Ok we're done, let's finalize. This is a React component library — I added the date picker component. No release automation set up, just npm publish manually. Tests are in Jest.
evals.expected_output: Graceful degradation: tests with coverage via Jest, code review, PII scan, conventional commit review, push, PR creation. Skips release monitoring, artifact install, and issue closure.

assertions.text: Runs tests via Jest with coverage
Skips release monitoring and artifact install phases
Still performs code review and PII scan

evals.prompt: Let's wrap this up and ship. Fixed the CSV parser bug from issue #7. I've got like 5 WIP commits that need cleaning up. The project uses pytest and has a GitHub Actions CI but no automated releases.
evals.expected_output: Tests with coverage via pytest, code review, PII scan, commit cleanup with user consent, push, PR with 'Closes #7'. Skips release/install phases. Closes issue #7.

assertions.text: Identifies the 5 WIP commits as needing cleanup
Asks user for consent before rewriting commit history
Runs tests via pytest with coverage
Skips release monitoring and artifact install
Still performs code review and PII scan
